summary: Package hijacks global console on import and exfiltrates all log output to attacker-controlled Telegram and Firebase endpoints
details: On require of this package, index.js synchronously loads 66o.js, which replaces the process-global `console` object with a Proxy. Every subsequent console.log/console.error/console.dir call in the installing application is intercepted and its arguments are sent via HTTP GET to a hardcoded Telegram Bot API endpoint (api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage) with hardcoded chat IDs, and console.dir payloads are additionally PUT to a hardcoded Firebase Realtime Database (iiilll.firebaseio.com). This is a one-way silent-relay data channel from the installer's runtime to attacker-controlled infrastructure, triggered simply by importing the module — no opt-in, no documented purpose, and the destinations are hardcoded. Additionally, index.js installs a `globalThis.E` object whose methods PUT arbitrary data to the same Firebase instance and upload images to a hardcoded Cloudinary account (cloud `o6`), providing a second exfiltration pathway. Any log statement that touches sensitive data (tokens, user input, errors containing secrets) in a process that imports this module will be leaked.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
